paragraph: Single-family homes dominate Ridgefield Park's market with 32 sales over eight months at a median of $625,000. Sellers are getting their ask or better, with 59 percent of homes selling above list and a sale-to-list ratio of 1.01, though median days on market sits at 35 days, which means priced homes are moving but not frantically. Multi-family properties tell a different story. Only seven sales, but they're selling faster and hotter. The median is $680,000, with 86 percent going over asking and a median of 14 days on market. This is a real seller's market for the multi-family tier, driven by limited inventory and strong buyer interest. Condos and townhouses are sparse with five sales at a median of $295,000. These sit longer at 49 days on market and only 40 percent sold above list, signaling that buyers have more room to negotiate in this category. Co-ops round out the market at an entry point of $177,250, though the sample is tiny at four sales. If you're weighing options in Ridgefield Park, the deciding factor isn't market heat. It's whether you want the space and grounds of a single-family or multi-family property, or the lower price point of a condo or co-op.
